title: 7-Day North Island Family Road Trip
subtitle: Auckland → Rotorua → Taupo → Wellington
tagline: NZ's classic North Island loop — geothermal parks, lakes, and the capital city
best_for: Families with kids 3+, first-time NZ visitors

days.title: Arrival in Auckland
activities: Pick up rental car from Auckland Airport
Drive into Auckland CBD (45 mins)
Kelly Tarlton's Sea Life Aquarium (if arriving early)
Walk the waterfront, dinner at the Viaduct
days.accommodation: Auckland CBD hotel or holiday park
tips: Book accommodation near the motorway for easy departure next morning
Countdown supermarkets are cheapest for groceries

days.title: Drive to Rotorua — Hobbiton Option
activities: Option A: Drive direct to Rotorua (2.5h), check in, afternoon at Te Puia
Option B: Hobbiton Movie Set detour in Matamata (add 1 hour each way, book ahead at $49/adult)
Evening: Dinner in Rotorua CBD
tips: Hobbiton MUST be pre-booked — often sold out weeks ahead
The direct route via Matamata is still beautiful

days.title: Geothermal & Farm Day
activities: Agrodome Farm Show (9:30am — book ahead)
Lunch break (Eat Streat Rotorua has great cheap options)
Skyline Rotorua Gondola & Luge (afternoon)
Kuirau Park free geothermal walk (evening)
tips: Get the multi-ride luge pass for better value
Kuirau Park has free footbaths — let the kids put their feet in

days.title: Wai-O-Tapu + Drive to Taupo
days.drive_from_prev: 1h (via Wai-O-Tapu detour — 30 mins south of Rotorua)
activities: Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland (arrive by 10am for Lady Knox Geyser at 10:15am)
Drive to Taupo (1h from Wai-O-Tapu)
Check in, then walk to Huka Falls (free, 15 mins from town)
Spa Thermal Park evening soak (free natural hot spring)
tips: Wai-O-Tapu's Lady Knox Geyser erupts at exactly 10:15am — don't miss it
Spa Thermal Park has no changing rooms — wear togs under clothes

days.title: Huka Jet + Lake Day
activities: Huka Jet boat experience (book ahead — 30 mins, leaves from near Huka Falls)
Picnic at Acacia Bay (great swimming beach)
Aratiatia Rapids (controlled release at 2pm and 4pm in summer — check times)
AC Baths thermal pools (affordable family swim)
tips: Buy groceries for your beach picnic — saves $40+ vs eating out
Aratiatia Rapids release times change seasonally — check the sign at the dam

days.title: Drive Day to Wellington
days.drive_from_prev: 4h direct (or split with Tongariro National Park stop)
activities: Option A: Drive direct to Wellington (4h), check in, walk the waterfront
Option B: Stop at Tongariro National Park for 1.5h (dramatic volcanic scenery — see the Chateau)
Cable car up to Kelburn (Wellington — $10 return, great views)
Dinner on Cuba Street
tips: The Tongariro/Desert Road (SH1) is dramatic but add 30 mins
Wellington waterfront has great free playgrounds

days.title: Wellington Day + Departure
activities: Te Papa Museum (free, world-class — kids love the earthquake simulator)
Wellington Cable Car (views over the harbour)
Zealandia (optional — native wildlife sanctuary, 2h)
Return rental car at Wellington Airport
days.accommodation: Fly home or continue to South Island (ferry or flight)
tips: Te Papa is free and easily fills 3 hours
Book the Interislander ferry in advance if continuing to South Island

affiliate_blocks.cta: Compare rental cars for this trip
affiliate_blocks.note: Manual/small SUV recommended for 7-day North Island trip

affiliate_blocks.cta: Get travel insurance for your NZ trip

packing_tips: Rain jacket for everyone — NZ weather changes fast
Swimwear and towels (you'll swim multiple times)
Reusable water bottles (save $20+/day vs buying water)
Snack bag for the car — petrol station food is expensive
Sunscreen — NZ UV is intense even on cloudy days

faqs.q: Can we do this itinerary in a campervan?
faqs.a: Yes, and it works even better. You save on accommodation ($40–80/night vs $150–350) and can cook your own meals. Campervans also carry everything you need. See our campervan comparison.

faqs.q: Is this itinerary suitable for toddlers?
faqs.a: Yes — most activities on this route work for under-3s. Agrodome, Te Puia, Huka Falls, and Te Papa are all great for toddlers. The Huka Jet requires minimum 3 years/90cm.

faqs.q: What's the best time of year for this trip?
faqs.a: December–February (summer) is peak season — warm, long days, everything open. January is school holidays (very busy). November and March are sweet spots — shoulder prices, fewer crowds, still warm.

seo_keywords: north island family road trip nz

title: 10-Day South Island Family Road Trip
subtitle: Christchurch → Kaikoura → Nelson → Westland → Queenstown → Wanaka
tagline: NZ's most spectacular drive — glaciers, seals, Alps, and the adventure capital
best_for: Families with kids 5+, those who have done the North Island before
transport: Rental car or campervan (campervan highly recommended)

days.title: Arrive Christchurch — Antarctic Centre
activities: Pick up rental car/campervan at Christchurch Airport
International Antarctic Centre (3–4 hours — do this first day, next to airport)
Botanic Gardens walk
Dinner in Christchurch CBD
tips: The Antarctic Centre is 500m from the airport — perfect Day 1 activity
Book penguin feeding time slot in advance

days.title: Drive to Kaikoura — Seals & Seafood
activities: Stop at Waipara wineries (30 mins north of Christchurch) for picnic and vineyard visit
Arrive Kaikoura — walk the Kaikoura Peninsula Walkway (seals everywhere, free)
Whale watching cruise (book well ahead — operates year-round)
Fresh crayfish dinner at Nin's Bin (roadside — the cheapest cray in NZ)
tips: Kaikoura's crayfish (crayfish/rock lobster) from Nin's Bin is iconic — get there early
Seal colony at the point is free and the seals are very close

days.title: Drive to Nelson via Marlborough
activities: Morning seal colony walk (free)
Drive via Blenheim — stop at a Marlborough vineyard (kids can explore while adults taste)
Arrive Nelson — Nelson Markets (Sat morning, world-class)
Queens Gardens and Tahuna Beach
tips: Nelson has NZ's most sunshine hours — great beach weather
Rabbit Island (20 mins) is a hidden gem for a swim

days.title: Abel Tasman National Park Day
activities: Water taxi to Split Apple Rock and beach snorkelling ($80/adult, $45/child)
Kayak tour of Abel Tasman (half-day from Marahau)
Totaranui Beach picnic (free, remote golden beach)
Return to Nelson for dinner
tips: Abel Tasman is a MUST — the golden sand beaches are unlike anywhere else in NZ
Book kayak tours well ahead in January–February

days.title: West Coast Drive — Pancake Rocks
activities: Farewell Spit detour (optional, add 1.5h — incredible sandspit at the top of the South Island)
Punakaiki Pancake Rocks & Blowholes (free — 30-min walk, spectacular)
Dolomite Point walk
Overnight at Punakaiki or push on to Greymouth
tips: Punakaiki blowholes are best at high tide — check tide times before visiting
The Pancake Rocks are free and spectacular — non-negotiable stop

days.title: West Coast to Glacier Country
activities: Hokitika Gorge detour (30 mins — stunning turquoise river, suspension bridge)
Hokitika town (jade/pounamu carving workshops)
Arrive Franz Josef — evening Glacier Valley Walk (free, 1.5h return)
tips: Hokitika Gorge is one of NZ's most Instagrammed spots — worth the detour
Franz Josef is tiny — book accommodation well ahead

days.title: Glacier Day
activities: Glacier helicopter experience ($300–500/person — NOT cheap but extraordinary)
OR Glacier Valley Walk (free — as close to the ice face as you can get on foot)
Peter's Pool Walk (mirror reflection of mountains)
Lake Matheson (Mirror Lake — Mt Cook/Mt Tasman reflection)
tips: Helicopter tours require good weather — have a backup plan
The Valley Walk to the glacier face is free and still incredible
Minimum age for helicopter is typically 3

days.title: Haast Pass — Most Dramatic Drive in NZ
activities: Drive Haast Pass — stop at Thunder Creek Falls and Blue Pools (free walkways)
Arrive Wanaka — Puzzling World
Lake Wanaka swim (Glendhu Bay)
Dinner in Wanaka town
tips: Haast Pass is one of the world's great drives — allow 5 hours minimum with stops
Blue Pools (near Makarora) have crystal-clear water — swim if warm enough

days.title: Drive to Queenstown — Adventure Day
activities: Crown Range road (highest sealed road in NZ — spectacular views, drive slowly)
Arrive Queenstown
Skyline Gondola & Luge (afternoon)
Queenstown waterfront evening and dinner
tips: Crown Range is stunning — stop at the viewpoint at the top
Book gondola online to save queuing

days.title: Final Day — Queenstown Activities + Fly Out
activities: Jet boat experience (Kawarau or Shotover — book ahead)
Arrowtown day trip (20 mins — historic gold-rush town, free)
Return rental car at Queenstown Airport
Fly home
tips: Return the car with a full tank of fuel — petrol at the airport is overpriced
Allow 2h before your flight for the airport

affiliate_blocks.cta: Compare campervans for the South Island
affiliate_blocks.note: Campervan HIGHLY recommended for this route — saves $800+ on accommodation

affiliate_blocks.cta: Get travel insurance — essential for NZ road trips

packing_tips: Warm layer for glacier country (Franz Josef can be cold even in summer)
Sandflies repellent (the West Coast has fierce sandflies — DEET essential)
Rain jacket (West Coast gets 7m of rain per year)
Swimwear and quick-dry towel (multiple swimming stops)
Groceries topped up at major towns (Christchurch, Nelson, Hokitika, Queenstown)

faqs.q: Should we do a campervan or rental car for the South Island?
faqs.a: Campervan, if budget allows. The South Island has world-class holiday parks and freedom camping spots. A 6-berth campervan for a family of 4 saves $600–1200 in accommodation over 10 days. Compare campervan prices above.

faqs.q: Is this route suitable for toddlers?
faqs.a: Yes with modifications. Toddlers will love the animals (seals at Kaikoura, Abel Tasman birds), the beaches, and the playgrounds. Skip the helicopter and jet boat if under 3. The driving days are long — break them with short walks and stops.

faqs.q: Can we drive this in winter?
faqs.a: With caution. The Haast Pass and Crown Range can be icy — check road conditions daily at nzta.govt.nz. The West Coast and Westland glaciers are actually spectacular in winter with fresh snow. Carry tyre chains if renting in June–August.

seo_keywords: south island family road trip nz

title: Queenstown & Wanaka Family Long Weekend
subtitle: 3–4 Days — Adventure + Lakes
tagline: The perfect South Island long weekend — adventure in Queenstown, calm in Wanaka
best_for: Families wanting a focused South Island getaway

days.title: Arrive Queenstown — Gondola Day
activities: Pick up rental car
Skyline Gondola & Luge (3+ hours)
Queenstown foreshore walk
Fergburger dinner (queue early)
tips: Book gondola online to skip queues
Buy multi-ride luge pass

days.title: Jet Boat + Lake Day
activities: Jet boat on the Kawarau River (Queenstown Jet)
Lunch at Frankton Arm
Queenstown Gardens frisbee golf (free)
TSS Earnslaw Steamship sunset cruise
tips: Jet boat minimum age: 3 years. Under that, do the Steamship instead.

days.title: Crown Range to Wanaka
activities: Crown Range scenic drive (stop at top viewpoint)
Arrive Wanaka — Puzzling World
Lake Wanaka swim at Glendhu Bay
Cinema Paradiso evening (book ahead)
tips: Crown Range has the best views in the South Island — stop at the top

days.title: Wanaka Morning + Fly Out
activities: Lake Wanaka morning walk (That Wanaka Tree photo)
Brunch at Federal Diner Wanaka
Drive to Queenstown Airport (1h)
Fly home
tips: Allow 2h before your flight — Queenstown Airport can be busy

packing_tips: Layers (Queenstown evenings are cool year-round)
Swimwear
Walking shoes

faqs.q: Is 4 days enough for Queenstown and Wanaka?
faqs.a: Yes — 2 nights in Queenstown and 1–2 in Wanaka covers the key experiences without rushing. You could add Arrowtown (20 mins from Queenstown) as a free half-day.

title: 5-Day Wellington to Nelson Family Road Trip
subtitle: Ferry crossing, NZ's best free museum, and Abel Tasman National Park
tagline: Two of NZ's most underrated destinations — linked by the spectacular Interislander ferry
best_for: Families who want culture and beaches without the Queenstown crowds
highlights: Abel Tasman water taxi beach day

days.title: Wellington City
activities: Te Papa Museum (3–4 hours)
Wellington Cable Car return
Walk back through Botanical Garden
Waterfront dinner
days.accommodation: Wellington CBD hotel or TOP 10 Holiday Park
tips: Book Te Papa night tours in advance
The cable car runs until 10pm — go at dusk for harbour views

days.title: Zealandia + Departure
activities: Zealandia morning visit (book ahead)
Midday Interislander Ferry (3h through Marlborough Sounds)
Arrive Picton — explore the waterfront
tips: Book the ferry well ahead — family compartments book out fast
Sit outside on the ferry for the Marlborough Sounds scenery
Kids love spotting dolphins from the deck

days.title: Drive to Nelson
activities: Stop at Pelorus Bridge Scenic Reserve (swimming hole, beautiful bush)
Nelson city arrival
Tahunanui Beach afternoon
Nelson Saturday Market if weekend
days.accommodation: Nelson city or Tahunanui Beach Holiday Park
tips: Pelorus Bridge swimming hole is one of NZ's best-kept secrets — don't skip it
Tahunanui Beach has a great playground right on the sand

days.title: Abel Tasman Day
activities: Water taxi from Kaiteriteri to Anchorage Bay
Beach day — swimming, snorkelling, exploring
Water taxi return
Mapua Wharf dinner (30 min from Nelson)
tips: Book water taxi 2+ weeks ahead in peak season
Bring snorkelling gear — the water is clear enough to see fish
Mapua Wharf has the best food in the region — fish and chips, craft beer, gelato

days.title: Departure Day
days.drive: Nelson to airport or continue south
activities: World of WearableArt museum (allow 2h)
Or: relaxed morning, café in Nelson
Fly home from Nelson Airport or continue to Christchurch via SH6 (4h 30m)
tips: WoW Museum is excellent and often overlooked — not just fashion
Nelson to Christchurch via SH6 through Murchison is a beautiful drive if extending your trip

affiliate_blocks.heading: Getting Around
affiliate_blocks.text: A rental car is essential for this itinerary. Pick up in Wellington and drop off in Nelson (one-way fee applies, typically $150–250).

affiliate_blocks.heading: The Interislander Ferry
affiliate_blocks.text: Book the ferry well ahead — family compartments and peak sailings sell out fast. Foot passenger or drive-on are both options.

packing_tips: Windproof jacket for Wellington
Snorkelling gear for Abel Tasman
Sunscreen — Nelson is NZ's sunniest region
Spade hire available at Hot Water Beach
Reusable water bottles — tap water is excellent throughout

faqs.q: Is the Interislander Ferry good for kids?
faqs.a: Kids love it. Three hours through the Marlborough Sounds, with a café, play areas, and the chance to spot dolphins and seals from the deck. One of the highlights of the trip.

faqs.q: Can you do this in reverse (Nelson to Wellington)?
faqs.a: Yes — just as good. Some families fly into Nelson and take the ferry back to Wellington at the end of the trip.

title: 4-Day Coromandel Family Road Trip from Auckland
subtitle: Hot Water Beach, Cathedral Cove, and the most beautiful beaches within reach of Auckland
tagline: Auckland's backyard — world-class beaches and geothermal wonders in 4 days
best_for: Auckland families, first-time Coromandel visitors, beach lovers
highlights: Hot Water Beach at low tide

days.title: Auckland to Thames to Coromandel Town
days.drive: Auckland to Coromandel Town (2h 30m via Thames)
activities: Karangahake Gorge windows walk (SH25, 45 min, free — go through the old gold mine tunnels)
Arrive Coromandel Town
Driving Creek Railway (book 2pm session)
Coromandel Smoking Company for dinner
days.accommodation: Coromandel Town holiday park or B&B
tips: Don't skip Karangahake Gorge — kids love the mine tunnels and it's free
Book Driving Creek Railway before you leave Auckland

days.title: Cathedral Cove + Hahei
activities: Cathedral Cove morning (walk 35 min each way or take the boat from Hahei)
Hahei Beach afternoon swim
Explore Hahei village
Fish and chips dinner
days.accommodation: Hahei Holiday Resort or Hot Water Beach TOP 10
tips: Cathedral Cove walk opens at 9am — go as early as possible to beat the crowds
The boat from Hahei Wharf ($45 adult/$23 child return) saves the walk if you have young kids

days.title: Hot Water Beach Day
activities: Hot Water Beach at low tide (check hotwatertide.co.nz the night before)
Hire spades ($5) and dig your hot pool
Afternoon: return to Hahei or explore Whitianga by ferry ($5 each way)
Whitianga waterfront and beach
tips: The Whitianga ferry is 5 minutes and $5 — a fun mini adventure for kids
Water's Edge restaurant in Whitianga is excellent for a nicer dinner

days.title: Drive Back to Auckland
activities: Morning swim at Hahei if the weather is good
Stop at Miranda Shorebird Centre on the way back (thousands of godwits from Alaska, free entry)
Or: stop at Thames for lunch and Butterfly and Orchid Garden ($15/adult, $8/child)
Back in Auckland by mid-afternoon
tips: Miranda is perfect for birdwatching — tens of thousands of migratory birds from August to March
The Thames to Auckland stretch can be slow on Sunday afternoons in summer — leave Hahei by 2pm

affiliate_blocks.heading: Getting There
affiliate_blocks.text: A car is essential for the Coromandel. Petrol is expensive on the peninsula — fill up in Thames before heading north.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Book activities
affiliate_blocks.text: Driving Creek Railway and Cathedral Cove boat both book out in peak season. Book before you leave Auckland.

packing_tips: Spade for Hot Water Beach (hire on-site for $5)
Check tide times before you go (hotwatertide.co.nz)
Sunscreen — Coromandel gets intense summer sun
Snorkelling gear for Cathedral Cove marine reserve
Cash — some smaller cafes are cash-only

faqs.q: Can you do Hot Water Beach and Cathedral Cove in one day?
faqs.a: Yes — they're only 10 minutes apart. Do Cathedral Cove in the morning, then Hot Water Beach around low tide in the afternoon. Check tide times the day before.

Parts of SH25A over the Kopu-Hikuai saddle are steep and winding — take it slowly with a campervan. The main SH25 coastal route is easy. From Thames north to Coromandel Town via SH25 is a beautiful coastal road.
